summary: Decompiled five item-related functions across Pokemon items, Oldkuri/Goomba, Lugia, and Kirby Cutter Beam, replacing placeholder comments with concrete C implementations and tightening related header prototypes. The diff adds item animation, physics, collision, and SpawnItem construction patterns. Evidence on matching is mixed: the PR body says all five functions 100% matched via an overnight script, while the decomp.dev bot report in the slice records 3 new 100% matches and 2 near-100% improvements.

key_files.role: Implements Blastoise/Kamex active water-gun animation/timer logic, hitlag callbacks, accessory callback restore, and motion update.
Implements Pikachu Thunder collision behavior, including ground/contact checks, positional offset from special attributes, Thunder teardown via it_802B22B8, and ftPikachu SpecialLw callbacks.
Implements Oldkuri/Goomba rolling physics, turn-state handling, velocity reset, floor-normal velocity adjustment, and gravity/physics update.
Implements Lugia-related item spawning from a stored previous position, current spawn position, facing direction, parent objects, and velocity returned by it_802D1F64.
Replaces UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed Item_GObj*, ItemKind, f32, and Vec3 signatures needed by the decompiled Lugia spawner.
Implements Kirby Cutter Beam item creation with full SpawnItem setup, owner links, debug hook, and post-spawn initialization.
Refines it_8029BB90 from unknown signature to void it_8029BB90(Item_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObj* owner).

what_changed: Replaced `/// #itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim` with a bool animation callback that checks `it_80272C6C`, decrements `xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.timer`, stops x velocity and transitions via `it_802CA938` when the timer expires, updates animation with `Item_80268E5C`, and restores `efLib_PauseAll`, `efLib_ResumeAll`, and `it_802CA8DC` callbacks.

Replaced `/// #itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll` with a bool collision callback that branches on `xDD4_itemVar.pikachuthunder.x0`, calls `it_8026DA70`, offsets a local Vec3 by `-attrs->x8` through `it_80272AC4`, calls `it_802B22B8`, and notifies Pikachu SpecialLw code through `ftPk_SpecialLw_CheckProperty` and `ftPk_SpecialLw_80127608` when an associated fighter object is present.

Replaced `/// #itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys` with rolling physics that uses `xDFC` plus `it_802750E8(gobj, 2)` to trigger a turn/update path, reads `itOldkuriAttributes`, stores horizontal speed in `xDF4`, zeroes Y/Z velocity, flips facing to -1.0f, clears `xDFC`, calls `it_802D848C`, then applies floor-normal velocity adjustment via `it_8027C8D0` and physics update via `it_8027C0A8`.

Replaced `/// #it_802D1E8C` in Lugia with a typed `Item_GObj*` returning SpawnItem constructor that reads previous position from `ip->xDD4_itemVar.lugia.x70_pad`, computes spawn position with `it_8026BB88`, copies facing direction, gets velocity from `it_802D1F64(gobj, param)`, sets parent fields, and returns `Item_80268B18(&spawn)`.

Replaced `/// #it_8029BAB8` with Kirby Cutter Beam spawning code that forces `pos->z = 0.0f`, sets `spawn.kind = It_Kind_Kirby_CBeam`, fills `prev_pos`, computes `spawn.pos` via `it_8026BB68`, zeroes damage and velocity, links parent gobj fields, sets `x44_flag.b0`, creates the item with `Item_80268B18`, then calls `it_8029BB90`, `db_80225DD8`, and `it_802750F8` if spawn succeeds.

Added or rearranged includes needed by the decompiled code, including `db/db.h`, `it/item.h`, `it/types.h`, and `it/it_266F.h`, while removing an obsolete `it/forward.h` include from `itkirbycutterbeam.c`.
Updated headers to replace unknown prototypes for functions now called with known argument and return types.
smart_moves: The PR did not only drop in function bodies; it also propagated type knowledge into headers, especially `it_8029BB90`, `it_802D1E8C`, and `it_802D1F64`, reducing future reliance on `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS`.

Both Kirby Cutter Beam and Lugia implementations use the standard `SpawnItem` setup pattern: fill kind, previous/current position, facing direction, damage, velocity, parent object fields, `x44_flag.b0`, `x40`, then call `Item_80268B18`.

The item spawn functions preserve item-specific post-spawn behavior instead of treating creation as generic: Kirby Cutter Beam additionally calls `it_8029BB90`, `db_80225DD8`, and `it_802750F8` on the spawned item.

The Kamex and Oldkuri functions keep small matching-sensitive details visible, including `PAD_STACK(8)`, nested `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj)` declarations, direct field access on `xDD4_itemVar`, and exact update helper calls.

The Pikachu Thunder collision implementation correctly bridges item state back to fighter special-state code through the existing `ftPk_SpecialLw_*` helpers rather than hiding that relationship behind an unknown call.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling item spawners, fully type the surrounding helpers and headers; a matching body often exposes the true `SpawnItem`, `Item_GObj*`, `ItemKind`, `Vec3`, and owner parameter flow.

Preserve initialization order and exact field writes in `SpawnItem` setup, including zeroing velocity components and setting parent fields like `x0_parent_gobj` and `x4_parent_gobj2`; these patterns recur across item projectile constructors.

Do not prematurely rename or restructure raw item variables when field meaning is still weak. This PR keeps `xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.timer`, `x68`, `x6C`, `oldkuri.xDF4`, `oldkuri.xDFC`, and `lugia.x70_pad` until better struct knowledge exists.
`PAD_STACK(8)` and local-variable scoping can be part of the matching solution for item callbacks; avoid simplifying them away unless the match is verified.
Attribute blobs may still require casts during decompilation, as shown by `((f32*) attr->x0)[1]` and `ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes` usage.

Verification evidence should be checked across sources: the PR body claims five 100% matches, but the bot report records only three exact new matches plus two near matches for Kamex and Pikachu Thunder.
naming_conventions: Known item motion callbacks use item names plus motion slot and callback kind, e.g. `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll`, and `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys`.
Still-unknown or address-named item functions remain in `it_802xxxxx` form, e.g. `it_802D1E8C`, `it_8029BAB8`, and `it_802D1F64`.
Headers retain address comments beside prototypes, such as `/* 2D1E8C */ Item_GObj* it_802D1E8C(Item_GObj*, ItemKind, f32);`.
Common item locals follow project conventions: `Item_GObj* gobj`, `Item* ip`, `SpawnItem spawn`, `Item_GObj* spawned_gobj`, `it...Attributes* attrs` or `attr`, and `facing_dir`.
Raw struct fields remain offset-style where semantics are unresolved, including `xDD4_itemVar`, `xC4_article_data`, `x4_specialAttributes`, `x40_vel`, `x44_flag.b0`, `xDF4`, `xDFC`, and `x70_pad`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)` appears in `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll`, indicating stack padding was part of these matches or near-matches.
Kamex starts with `Item* ip = gobj->user_data` but later redeclares `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj)` inside a nested block, a scoping pattern worth preserving when matching similar item animation code.
Oldkuri also redeclares `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj)` inside the conditional block before reading attributes, likely preserving register or stack lifetime behavior.

SpawnItem construction uses explicit scalar writes rather than broad memset-style initialization; examples include `spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0f`, `spawn.x3C_damage = 0`, and `spawn.x40 = 0`.
Lugia relies on a return-by-value `Vec3` helper prototype for `it_802D1F64`, enabling the exact assignment `spawn.vel = it_802D1F64(gobj, param)`.
The Lugia previous-position source is expressed as `*(Vec3*) ip->xDD4_itemVar.lugia.x70_pad`, showing that pointer casts to partially understood item-var storage can be necessary for matching.
Kirby Cutter Beam sets `pos->z = 0.0f` before copying `*pos` into `spawn.prev_pos`, so call-site-visible mutation and copy order matter.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code at 59.38% (+0.02%, +640 bytes). It listed 3 new 100% matches: `it_8029BAB8`, `it_802D1E8C`, and `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys`.
Reported 3 improvements in unmatched items, including `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll` at 99.80%, `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim` at 99.44%, and `itkirbycutterbeam` `.sdata2` at 12.50%.
Author stated all 5 functions were 100% matched and verified by an overnight script; this conflicts with the bot percentages available in the slice.
No human review comments or review records were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or commits that finish matching `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim` and `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll` if the decomp.dev near-match report was accurate.
Search for other item spawner decompilations using `SpawnItem`, `Item_80268B18`, `it_8026BB68`, or `it_8026BB88` to reuse the initialization order.
Search for remaining `itlugia.h` `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes near `it_802D208C` and Lugia logic functions.
Search for Pokemon item callbacks using `xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.timer`, `efLib_PauseAll`, and `efLib_ResumeAll`.
Search for `PAD_STACK(8)` in item motion callbacks to identify similar stack-sensitive matching patterns.
Search for fighter-special callbacks from item collision code, especially `ftPk_SpecialLw_*` interactions.
